delivered.compositionBenchmark: 20 real-life tasks, 100% deterministic composition (missing bricks honestly flagged)
delivered.modelDrivenPatchLoop: Plan -> risk-assess -> isolated apply -> build/test -> rollback -> knowledge proposal (fake-model tested)
delivered.gpuAwareOrchestration: GPU signal detection (no hard dep) + run queue + budget + bounded retry + telemetry + graceful fallback
delivered.pythonBridge: Safe C# bridge (allowlisted entrypoints, JSON I/O, timeout, graceful when Python absent) + stdlib worker skeleton
delivered.knowledgeGrowthScraper: Allowlist + cache-by-hash + citation + dedup + clean-room summarise + human-approval proposals

honest: A genuine, tested V2 core. NOT delivered as full per-component depth: LearningLoop is composed from existing pieces (model-driven runner already proposes knowledge + stores experience) rather than a standalone module; the model-driven loop is tested with a FAKE planner (no live model); Python workers are a stdlib skeleton (full ML/scrape workers need a local venv). Per-component test targets (40-60 each) not all met.
classificationReached: LAF_SOFTWARE_REASONING_ENGINE_V2_LOCAL_CORE_READY (core delivered + composition proven); stretch agent classification NOT reached (the model-driven loop is a safe validated core, not an unattended autonomous agent).
